claim: Anthropic enforced a policy banning third-party harnesses (like OpenClaw) from drawing against Claude subscriptions with less than 24 hours notice
actionable: Avoid dependency on Claude subscriptions for multi-harness workflows; maintain multi-model strategies to escape vendor lock-in

claim: Switching costs between frontier models are effectively zero; switching Claude to GPT in OpenClaw took 3 minutes with prompt file updates
actionable: Build abstraction layers over model selection; maintain prompt variations for different models to enable rapid switching

claim: Anthropic's GPU crunch is severe (uptime below 99% on claude.ai); their $30B revenue run rate exceeds OpenAI's, but capacity cannot keep pace
actionable: Expect continued service disruptions and quota limits from Anthropic; maintain fallback models in agent workflows

claim: OpenAI acquired Peter Steinberg (OpenClaw lead) and is explicitly courting OpenClaw users; GPT-5.4 personality is now tuned to feel like Claude
actionable: OpenClaw users should migrate to GPT-5.4; OpenAI is investing in multi-harness compatibility while Anthropic is retreating

claim: Anthropic is blocking prompts that declare agent identity (e.g., 'running inside OpenClaw') as a classifier defense; this was unintentional but signals fragility
actionable: Don't rely on prompt-based workarounds to escape vendor policies; expect classifiers to evolve and break such hacks

claim: Multi-model strategies should include open-source models for classification, extraction, and summarization to reduce dependency on frontier model APIs
actionable: Offload low-value tasks to open-source models; use frontier models only for high-value reasoning and coding
